chapters.title: Analytic Geometry
milestones: ch03/: 2 notebooks, geometry.py, tests, README. SSW Ch 1 notes.

chapters.title: Decompositions
milestones: ch04/: 4 notebooks (incl image compression), decompositions.py. SSW Ch 2 notes.

chapters.title: Vector Calculus
milestones: ch05/: 3 notebooks, calculus.py, tests, README. SSW Ch 3-5 notes.

chapters.title: Probability (compressed)
milestones: ch06/: 3 notebooks, probability.py, smoke tests, README. SSW Ch 6 notes. (compressed)

chapters.title: Optimization (compressed)
milestones: ch07/: 2 notebooks, optimizers.py, smoke tests, README. SSW phase 1 summary. (compressed)

tasks.description: Read all of GT ILA Ch 1: Systems of Linear Equations, Row Reduction, Parametric Form. Play with every interactive demo. Do embedded exercises.
Finish Ch 1 exercises if any remain. Start GT Ch 2: Vectors, Vector Equations and Spans, Matrix Equations. Focus on geometric interpretation.
tasks.ptTasks: Tensor creation (torch.tensor, zeros, ones, rand, arange). Dtypes, shapes, devices. Basic operations: add, mul, matmul. Reshaping: view, reshape, permute, squeeze, unsqueeze.

tasks.description: Continue GT Ch 2: Solution Sets, Linear Independence, Subspaces. Linear independence section is critical. Spend extra time on the interactive demos.
tasks.ptTasks: Indexing and slicing. Reproducibility (manual_seed). Device-agnostic code (cuda/cpu). NumPy <-> tensor conversion. Run all code on your RTX 5070 Ti.

tasks.description: Finish GT Ch 2: Basis and Dimension, Bases as Coordinate Systems, The Rank Theorem. Rank Theorem ties everything together. Do all exercises.
tasks.ptTasks: Re-do tensor operations from memory without looking. Create a cheat sheet of the 10 most-used tensor ops. If comfortable, start reading Ch 01.

tasks.description: Start GT Ch 3: Matrix Transformations, One-to-one and Onto, Linear Transformations. Understand what a linear map preserves.
tasks.ptTasks: Create synthetic linear data. Train/test split. Build nn.Module subclass (LinearRegressionModel). Understand parameters, forward(). Loss fn (L1Loss, MSELoss) and optimizer (SGD).

tasks.topic: Matrix Multiplication, Inverses, Invertible Matrix Thm
tasks.description: Finish GT Ch 3: Matrix Multiplication, Matrix Inverses, Invertible Matrix Theorem. The IMT is the single most important theorem connecting all Ch 2 concepts.
tasks.ptTasks: THE core loop: forward pass, loss, zero_grad, backward, step. Train 100+ epochs. Plot loss curves. Evaluate with inference_mode(). Save/load with state_dict. MEMORIZE this loop.

tasks.description: Read MML 2.4 opening pages on groups/vector space axioms (20 min). Read MML 2.8 on affine spaces/mappings (30 min). Key takeaway: y = Wx + b is affine, not linear.
tasks.ptTasks: Write a complete training loop from scratch, no reference. If you cannot do this cold, repeat until you can. This is the single most important skill.

tasks.description: Work through MML exercises 2.1-2.3 on paper. These test concepts you read in GT ILA but with MML notation. Bring wrong answers to Claude.
tasks.ptTasks: Make circles dataset. Build model with nn.Linear layers. BCEWithLogitsLoss. Sigmoid for predictions. See why linear model fails on nonlinear data. Add nn.ReLU layers.

tasks.topic: MML Exercises: Vector Spaces, Independence, Rank
tasks.description: Exercises 2.4-2.6. Focus on rank computation and proving/disproving linear independence. Hardest exercises in the set.
tasks.ptTasks: Multi-class with softmax + CrossEntropyLoss. Decision boundaries. Accuracy metric. Classification evaluation: confusion matrix concept. Train/test loop with accuracy tracking.

tasks.description: Finish exercises 2.7-2.9. These cover linear mappings and affine spaces. Write 1-page summary of Ch 2 in your own words.
tasks.ptTasks: Build a classifier from scratch on a new toy dataset. Experiment: what happens with too few/many hidden units? Too high/low learning rate? Document findings.

tasks.description: Code matmul with nested loops. Verify against numpy @. Handle dimension mismatch errors.
tasks.ptTasks: torchvision.datasets.FashionMNIST. DataLoader with batching. Baseline: flatten + linear model. Understand why CNNs exist (spatial structure, parameter sharing).

tasks.description: Input: augmented matrix. Output: row echelon form. Handle zero pivots with row swaps. Test 3x3 and 4x4.
tasks.ptTasks: Build TinyVGG: Conv2d -> ReLU -> Conv2d -> ReLU -> MaxPool2d -> flatten -> Linear. Understand kernel size, stride, padding. Train on FashionMNIST.

tasks.description: Implement inverse (Gauss-Jordan), rank (count pivots), null_space, col_space, is_independent. Test A @ inv(A) = I.
tasks.ptTasks: Compare baseline vs CNN: accuracy, loss, training time. Confusion matrix. Predictions on random samples. CPU vs GPU timing. functorch/torchinfo for model summary.

tasks.description: Implement change_of_basis(). Capstone: random 5x3, verify rank + nullity = n_cols over 100 trials. Test edge cases: zero, identity, singular matrices.
tasks.ptTasks: Rebuild TinyVGG from memory. Experiment: change kernel size, add layers, change pooling. What improves accuracy? What hurts it?

tasks.description: Refactor all code into clean linalg.py with docstrings: matmul, gauss_elim, rank, inverse, null_space, col_space, change_basis, is_independent.
tasks.ptTasks: Download pizza/steak/sushi data. torchvision.datasets.ImageFolder. Compose transforms (Resize, ToTensor, Normalize). Visualize transformed images. DataLoader setup.

tasks.description: pytest tests for linalg.py. Cover: identity, singular, rectangular, zero matrix, known solutions. Minimum 10 test cases.
tasks.ptTasks: Build custom Dataset subclass (__init__, __len__, __getitem__). Data augmentation (RandomHorizontalFlip, RandomRotation, TrivialAugmentWide). Train TinyVGG on custom data.

tasks.description: Add markdown + LaTeX to all 4 notebooks. Each tells a story: what, why, how, verify. Someone reading should learn from them.
tasks.ptTasks: Create a custom Dataset for a different image folder. Experiment with augmentation combos. Profile data loading speed with different num_workers.

tasks.description: Without notes: define vector space, basis, rank, null space, independence, affine space. If any is fuzzy, re-read.
tasks.ptTasks: Convert notebook to .py scripts: data_setup.py (DataLoaders), model_builder.py (TinyVGG), engine.py (train/test step), train.py (entry point), utils.py (save model). Run training from CLI.

tasks.description: Write README: concepts, implementations, ML connections (embeddings = vector spaces, rank = information, Wx+b = affine).
tasks.ptTasks: Modularize one of YOUR earlier notebooks into scripts. Verify it trains identically. This is professional practice you already know from Schwab; should be fast.

tasks.description: Visual demos: 2D/3D vector ops, span visualization, rank-deficient matrices. matplotlib plots.
tasks.ptTasks: torchvision.models (EfficientNet_B0). Freeze base layers. Replace classifier head. Feature extraction: only train new head. Compare vs TinyVGG from scratch.

tasks.description: Run all tests. All notebooks execute clean. Update top-level README.
tasks.ptTasks: Unfreeze some/all layers for fine-tuning. Learning rate scheduling. Compare: from-scratch vs feature-extract vs fine-tune. Plot results. Predict on custom images.

tasks.description: Read GT ILA Ch 6 section 1: Dot Products and Orthogonality. Interactive demos for geometric meaning. Cosine similarity = NLP embedding similarity.
tasks.ptTasks: Try a different pretrained model (ResNet, ViT). Summarize all chapters in 1 page. PyTorch fundamentals complete. Full focus shifts to MML.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW Ch 1.1 Intro: What is Learning. Skim 1.2 When to use ML. Light intro day.

tasks.sections: GT Ch 6: Orth Complements + Projection + Orth Sets
tasks.description: Read GT ILA Ch 6 sections 2-4: Orthogonal Complements, Orthogonal Projection, Orthogonal Sets. Projection is the most important concept. Gram-Schmidt.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW Ch 1.4 Learning Types. Take notes on supervised vs unsupervised framing.

tasks.sections: GT Ch 6: Least Squares + MML 3.1-3.4 gaps
tasks.description: Finish GT Ch 6: Least Squares. Skim MML 3.1-3.4 for any notation or concepts GT missed (inner product spaces beyond R^n). Light day.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW Notation section. Compare to MML notation. Note differences.

tasks.description: Implement dot_product, norm, angle_between, cosine_similarity. Verify angle(v,v)=0, angle(v,-v)=pi.
tasks.sswTasks: Re-read Ch 1 end-to-end. Make sure framing is solid before Ch 2 (PAC).

tasks.description: Implement project_onto_subspace and gram_schmidt. Verify: residual orthogonal, output orthonormal.
tasks.sswTasks: Summary notes on Ch 1 (1 page max). What does Shalev-Shwartz mean by "learning"?

tasks.description: Capstone: 3 random vectors in R5, Gram-Schmidt, verify pairwise dots ~0. Extract geometry.py. Tests.
tasks.sswTasks: Start SSW Ch 2.1 A Formal Model: Statistical Learning Framework. PAC intuition.

tasks.description: ch03/README.md. Final commit.
tasks.sswTasks: Continue SSW Ch 2.1-2.2. Realize how PAC bounds the gap between empirical and true risk.

tasks.description: Read all of GT ILA Ch 4: Definition, Cofactor Expansions, Determinants and Volumes. det=0 means singular. Volume interpretation.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW 2.2 Empirical Risk Minimization. Concept of ERM rule.

tasks.description: Finish GT Ch 4 exercises. Start GT Ch 5: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors, Characteristic Polynomial. Core definition: Av = lambda*v.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW 2.3 ERM with Inductive Bias. Why we restrict hypothesis class.

tasks.description: Finish GT Ch 5: Similarity, Diagonalization, Complex Eigenvalues, Stochastic Matrices. A = PDP^-1 and what it means.
tasks.sswTasks: Continue 2.3. Finite hypothesis class theorem. First real PAC bound.

tasks.description: Read MML 4.4 carefully. A = U Sigma V^T. Rotate-scale-rotate. This is the most important decomposition in ML and GT ILA does not cover it.
tasks.sswTasks: Try SSW exercises 2.1, 2.2. Bring stuck points to Claude.

tasks.topic: Matrix Approximation & PCA (NOT in GT ILA)
tasks.description: Read MML 4.5. Low-rank approx via SVD. Eckart-Young theorem. PCA = project onto top-k singular vectors.
tasks.sswTasks: SSW exercise 2.3. Verify ERM bound holds for your toy hypothesis class.

tasks.description: MML exercises 4.1-4.6. Compute SVD of 2x3 matrix by hand. Find eigenvalues of 3+ matrices. These are hard.
tasks.sswTasks: Summary notes on Ch 2. Why is ERM + finite class = learnable? 1-page write-up.

tasks.description: Finish exercises. Draw flow: determinants -> eigenvalues -> SVD -> PCA.
tasks.sswTasks: Start SSW Ch 3.1 PAC Learning. Formal definition. Read slowly.

tasks.description: Implement power iteration for dominant eigenvector. Observe convergence. Compare with numpy.linalg.eig.
tasks.sswTasks: Continue 3.1. PAC learnability definition. epsilon, delta meaning.

tasks.description: SVD via: form A^T A, eigendecompose, construct U/Sigma/V^T. Verify A = U @ Sigma @ V^T vs numpy.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW 3.2. Agnostic PAC learning (no realizable assumption).

tasks.description: Center data, covariance matrix, eigendecompose, project top-k. numpy arrays only.
tasks.sswTasks: Continue 3.2. Uniform convergence intuition.

tasks.description: Load Iris. YOUR PCA: 4D to 2D. Plot by species. Compare vs sklearn PCA. Diff < 1e-10.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW 3.3 Learning via Uniform Convergence. Connect to ERM.

tasks.description: Grayscale image. SVD reconstruct k=5,20,50,100. Side-by-side. Quality vs compression.
tasks.sswTasks: SSW exercises 3.1, 3.2. Pen and paper.

tasks.description: Extract decompositions.py. Write tests. Polish all notebooks with markdown + LaTeX.
tasks.sswTasks: SSW exercise 3.3 + remaining. Focus on PAC bound derivations.

tasks.description: ch04/README.md with PCA scatter plot. Final commit.
tasks.sswTasks: Summary: PAC vs agnostic PAC vs uniform convergence. 1-page diagram.

tasks.description: Read 5.1. Quick review: derivative, product/chain rule. Move fast if comfortable.
tasks.sswTasks: Skim SSW 4.1 Uniform Convergence is Sufficient. Get high-level idea only.

tasks.description: Read 5.2. THE most important section for deep learning. Gradient = steepest ascent. Work through ALL examples.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW 4.2 Finite Classes are Agnostic PAC Learnable. The big theorem.

tasks.description: Read 5.3. f: R^n -> R^m has Jacobian shape m x n. Each entry is a partial derivative.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW Appendix B on Hoeffding inequality. Reference for later. Light skim.

tasks.description: Read 5.4. Matrix calculus: derivatives of trace, det, matrix products. Appears in every ML paper.
tasks.sswTasks: Summary: Why uniform convergence => learnability. Save proof details for ML 391L.

tasks.description: Read 5.5-5.6. Composing Jacobians through a computational graph IS backpropagation. Non-negotiable.
tasks.sswTasks: Start SSW Ch 5.1 The No-Free-Lunch Theorem. Read carefully.

tasks.description: All chapter exercises. Priority: chain rule on multivariate compositions. Jacobians by hand.
tasks.sswTasks: Finish NFL theorem reading. Implication for hypothesis class choice.

tasks.description: Draw 2-layer NN. Forward pass as composition. Chain rule for dL/dW at each layer. PEN AND PAPER.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW 5.2 Error Decomposition: bias + variance + irreducible.

tasks.description: Finite differences: f'(x) ~ (f(x+h)-f(x-h))/2h. Test on sin, exp, polynomials.
tasks.sswTasks: Continue 5.2. Approximation error vs estimation error tradeoff.

tasks.description: gradient(f, x) for f: R^n -> R. jacobian(f, x) for f: R^n -> R^m. Finite differences. Verify.
tasks.sswTasks: Connect SSW Ch 5 ideas to MML Ch 5 (vector calculus). Optimization affects estimation error.

tasks.description: 2-layer NN: h=relu(X@W1+b1), y=h@W2+b2, L=MSE. Compute dL/dW analytically. Compare vs PyTorch autograd.
tasks.sswTasks: SSW exercise 5.1. Bias-complexity tradeoff problem.

tasks.description: If gradients match: add complexity. If not: trace chain rule step by step until match.
tasks.sswTasks: SSW exercise 5.2 if time permits. Otherwise wrap Ch 5.

tasks.description: Gradient vector fields for 2D functions. Overlay arrows on loss surface contour plots.
tasks.sswTasks: Summary: NFL + bias-complexity. Why no universal learner. 1-page write-up.

tasks.description: Extract calculus.py (numerical_gradient, jacobian, gradient_check). Tests. Polish notebooks.
tasks.sswTasks: Skim SSW Ch 6.1 Restriction of finite class. Concept only.

tasks.description: ch05/README.md with backprop diagram.
tasks.sswTasks: Read SSW 6.2 VC Dimension definition. Examples on lines, intervals.

tasks.description: Read MML 6.1 (sample space, events, axioms, conditional probability, independence) AND 6.2 (Bernoulli, Binomial, Gaussian, PDF/PMF/CDF) in one day. Focus on Gaussian.
tasks.sswTasks: Continue SSW 6.2 VC examples: rectangles, halfspaces. Pen + paper.

tasks.description: Read MML 6.3 (Bayes: prior, likelihood, posterior, evidence) AND 6.4 (Multivariate Gaussian, mean vector, covariance PSD) together.
tasks.sswTasks: Skim SSW 6.3 The Fundamental Theorem of Statistical Learning. Statement only.

tasks.description: Read MML 6.5-6.6. Derive Gaussian MLE by hand. MSE loss = Gaussian MLE. MAP = MLE + prior = regularization. Most important section for ML 391L.
tasks.sswTasks: Summary of VC dimension intuition. Save proofs for ML 391L.

tasks.description: Pick 4 highest-value MML exercises: one on Bayes, one on Gaussian MLE derivation, one on Bernoulli MLE, one on multivariate Gaussian. Skip the rest.
tasks.sswTasks: Note connection: VC bounds generalization, MLE chooses within hypothesis class.

tasks.description: Implement Gaussian class (pdf, log_pdf, sample, fit_mle, no scipy). Then Bayesian posterior with Gaussian prior + likelihood (conjugate). Plot prior/likelihood/posterior.
tasks.sswTasks: Re-read your own 1-page summaries of SSW Ch 1-6. Strengths/gaps.

tasks.description: Logistic regression via gradient ascent on log-likelihood. 2D toy data. Add Gaussian prior on weights to get MAP = L2 regularized. Compare boundaries.
tasks.sswTasks: Make a glossary of SSW terms: PAC, APAC, ERM, VC dim, UC, NFL, realizable, agnostic.

tasks.description: Extract probability.py with key classes. Skip writing exhaustive tests (just smoke tests). Write README connecting: cross-entropy = Bernoulli MLE, MSE = Gaussian MLE, L2 = Gaussian prior. Commit.
tasks.deliverable: probability.py + ch06/README.md + git push Ch 6. Tag: v0.5-probability
tasks.sswTasks: Quick self-test: define each glossary term cold. Re-read sections that are fuzzy.

tasks.description: Read MML 7.1 (optimization framework: objective, constraints) AND 7.2 (vanilla gradient descent, learning rate, convergence) together. Too big = diverge, too small = stuck.
tasks.sswTasks: Compile all SSW summaries into one doc. Save for ML 391L reference.

tasks.description: Read MML 7.3 in full: convex sets/functions, convexity guarantees global min, Lagrange multipliers, KKT overview. MSE convex, NN not.
tasks.sswTasks: Plan next phase: SSW Ch 7+ during Fall semester, deeper review before Spring 2027.

tasks.description: Read MML 7.4 + skim supplementary. Mini-batch SGD, momentum, Adam. SGD noise = implicit regularization.
tasks.sswTasks: Map: SSW concepts <-> MML chapters. E.g., SSW Ch 2 ERM <-> MML Ch 7 optimization.

tasks.description: 5 iterations of GD on f(x)=x^2+2x+1 with lr=0.1, 0.5, 1.5 by hand. Observe convergence/divergence. Prove one convexity statement.
tasks.sswTasks: Use this day to catch up on anything you missed in SSW Ch 1-6.

tasks.description: GD for f: R^n -> R. Track loss. Test on quadratic and Rosenbrock. Then SGD with mini-batches for linear regression. Compare convergence.
tasks.sswTasks: Continue catch-up or get ahead on SSW Ch 7 (Linear Predictors) if comfortable.

tasks.description: SGD+momentum and Adam (m/v accumulators, bias correction). Compare all 4 optimizers (GD, SGD, Momentum, Adam) on logistic regression. Loss curves.
tasks.sswTasks: SSW phase 1 (foundations) complete. Plan: continue SSW during Fall semester light reading.

tasks.description: Extract optimizers.py (GD, SGD, Momentum, Adam classes). Smoke tests. README with optimizer comparison chart. Final commit. DONE with MML prep.
tasks.deliverable: optimizers.py + ch07/README.md + git push Ch 7. Tag: v0.6-optimization. MML COMPLETE.
tasks.sswTasks: MML + SSW phase 1 both done. 4 weeks buffer before Fall classes start Aug 25. Rest.
